reasoning: The input is a news article about Wendy Houvenaghel and broader concerns about athlete welfare versus a win-at-all-costs culture in British Cycling. The task appears to be producing a concise summary of the piece, capturing the key revelations, reactions, and upcoming reforms.
output: Olympic silver medallist Wendy Houvenaghel has accused British Cycling of ageism and prioritising medals over athlete welfare, joining other cyclists who have criticised the World Class programme. A leaked draft report alleges the organisation sanitised an investigation into Shane Sutton’s sexist language toward Jess Varnish and admits failures to protect staff and athletes. Sutton and former chair Sir Dave Brailsford have since left British Cycling. Houvenaghel described the London 2012 selection as traumatic, recounting a culture of fear and a “medals at any cost” mindset that sidelined her partly due to age. A government-commissioned review into safety and wellbeing in sport, led by Baroness Grey-Thompson, is due to publish soon with reforms expected. While some peers have defended British Cycling’s leadership, Dame Sarah Storey cautioned that there are no excuses for crossing the line into bullying, emphasising the need to balance performance with athlete welfare.
